description_long: This object is a vintage-style iron hammer with a distinctive hexagonal base and a thick, solid body made of dark, rusted metal. The head features a serrated, toothed edge along the bottom, suggesting it may be a specialized tool, possibly for crushing or pressing rather than traditional hammering. The handle is short, cylindrical, and made of wood, attached to the body with a V-shaped metal support. The surface of the metal is heavily oxidized, giving it a mottled, brownish-black appearance with visible rust and wear. The overall form is bulky and robust, with a clear separation between the upper and lower halves of the body. This tool would likely be found in an urban garage, workshop, or as a decorative piece referencing historical tools.
description: A vintage iron hammer with a hexagonal, rusted metal body and a short wooden handle, featuring a serrated lower edge. The tool is robust, heavily worn, and has a unique, industrial appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the hexagonal face, the wooden handle centered on top, and the visible gap between the upper and lower halves.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the serrated, toothed edge along the bottom and the V-shaped metal support for the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the solid back of the hammer and the handle's attachment, with less visibility of the serrated edge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the serrated edge and the robust, rusted metal construction.
attribute_handle_type: Short, straight wooden handle with a smooth, slightly contoured grip and a V-shaped metal support.
attribute_head_type: Hexagonal iron head with a serrated, toothed lower edge, resembling a vintage pressing or crushing tool rather than a standard hammer.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible rust, mottled oxidation, and surface wear throughout the metal body.
